towns.Ang Mo Kio: (North-East) One of Singapore's original new towns, mature and well-served, with an MRT interchange and a famously walkable town centre.
towns.Bedok: (East) A huge, established eastern town near the coast, with deep amenities and quick access to the airport and East Coast.
towns.Bishan: (Central) A central, sought-after town with top schools and an MRT interchange, where flats command a premium for the location.
towns.Bukit Batok: (West) A green, settled western town beside nature reserves, with solid value and improving connectivity.
towns.Bukit Merah: (Central) A central, near-city town stretching from Tiong Bahru to Telok Blangah, with strong demand and a city-fringe premium.
towns.Bukit Panjang: (West) A north-western town on the LRT and Downtown Line, beside the nature reserves, offering value for the space.
towns.Bukit Timah: (Central) A prime, green central district known for top schools and landed housing, with limited and pricey HDB stock.
towns.Central Area: (Central) The downtown core itself, the rarest and priciest place to hold an HDB flat, steps from the CBD.
towns.Choa Chu Kang: (West) An affordable far-western town on the LRT, popular with families wanting space for the money.
towns.Clementi: (West) A well-connected western town next to one-north and the universities, with strong, steady demand.
towns.Geylang: (Central) A central, characterful district close to the city, with strong rental demand and city-fringe pricing.
towns.Hougang: (North-East) A large, settled north-eastern town on the LRT, offering family-friendly value with decent connectivity.
towns.Jurong East: (West) The west's regional centre, a major commercial hub with an MRT interchange and big redevelopment plans.
towns.Jurong West: (West) A large, affordable western town with deep amenities, popular with families on a budget.
towns.Kallang-Whampoa: (Central) A central, near-city town along the Kallang River, mixing older estates with strong connectivity.
towns.Marine Parade: (East) A mature coastal estate by East Coast Park, distinctive for having no MRT until recently and a settled, seaside character.
towns.Pasir Ris: (East) A relaxed far-eastern town by the beach and park, family-friendly with a fresher feel than most mature estates.
towns.Punggol: (North-East) Singapore's youngest waterfront town, with the newest flats, long leases and a fast-growing digital district.
towns.Queenstown: (Central) Singapore's oldest town, now a prized central estate near one-north and the city, with a strong premium.
towns.Sembawang: (North) An affordable northern coastal town with newer flats and long leases, popular for value.
towns.Sengkang: (North-East) A young, family-heavy north-eastern town on the LRT, with newer flats and long leases.
towns.Serangoon: (North-East) A central-leaning north-eastern town with an MRT interchange, good schools and the NEX hub.
towns.Tampines: (East) A big, fully grown estate in the east built around a regional town centre, three train stations and affordable family flats.
towns.Toa Payoh: (Central) A classic central town with an MRT interchange, a vibrant town centre and strong, steady demand.
towns.Woodlands: (North) The north's regional centre and gateway to Malaysia, affordable and set for a big connectivity upgrade.
towns.Yishun: (North) A large, affordable northern town with deep amenities and improving connectivity.
